results.first_line: TLDR: Hudson AI intern with real IA/user-flow renewal exposure, but senior product ownership for Podo is not proven.
TLDR: SI-style UI/UX designer with Hana/Samsung/public digital-twin projects, but little consumer growth evidence.
TLDR: Senior cross-discipline designer with SaaS dashboard and AI-tool impact, but may skew broad design leadership over mobile app product craft.
TLDR: Promising junior portfolio with health-app research and clear problem framing, but evidence looks mostly academic/personal.
TLDR: Relevant ecommerce/web-mobile designer with concrete business results, but product depth is thin in the extract.
TLDR: Good Podo-relevant candidate because she has both award-winning app work and a location-based social matching project.
TLDR: Strong visual/promotion production for LG ecommerce, but weak evidence for product UX ownership.
TLDR: Solid UX/UI candidate with VOC-driven service improvement and measurable inquiry lift, though domain is not social/subscription.
TLDR: Strong product UIUX candidate with AI service metrics and design-system cleanup, but no clear dating/social exposure.
TLDR: Relevant because her portfolio explicitly tackles 2030 couple communication/date-cost pain points, but much of it may be personal work.
TLDR: Broad UI/brand/character designer, but the extract does not show enough product-design evidence for Podo.
TLDR: High-fit product designer with strong platform/funnel metrics, though the domain is sports booking rather than language/social.
TLDR: Useful agency UIUX designer with web/mobile breadth and dashboard work, but limited consumer-product metrics.
TLDR: Low-confidence fit; evidence is mostly training, tools, and aspiration rather than shipped UIUX work.
TLDR: Strong product-thinking candidate with retention research and design-system work; likely worth a screen.
TLDR: Very strong commerce/product candidate with conversion gains, design-system leverage, and creator-supporter funding experience.
TLDR: Strong B2B SaaS/admin product designer with AI workflow and measurable subscription/legal-entity growth, but less direct consumer-social evidence.
TLDR: Early UIUX candidate with empathetic research instincts, but too junior for the senior Podo bar.
TLDR: This appears to duplicate 최윤정's resume text, so the card should be checked before evaluating separately.
TLDR: Senior visual/BX designer with some UIUX work for LG/KB, but likely not a focused product designer.
TLDR: Strong systems/data candidate who can make UX measurable and easier to ship; consumer-social fit still unclear.
TLDR: Very strong monetization/community candidate with creator purchase, subscription, and revenue metrics.
TLDR: Relevant AI/UX candidate with early conversion evidence, but likely needs seniority validation.
TLDR: Solid fintech UIUX/system designer with complex-data experience, but no direct social or subscription signal.
TLDR: Very experienced visual/BX designer, but evidence is much stronger for branding/graphics than product UX.
TLDR: Strong commerce UX candidate with concrete purchase-conversion and retention metrics.
TLDR: Junior-to-mid UX/UI candidate with full-process practice and pet-service research, but limited production proof.
TLDR: Strong AI/B2B SaaS product designer with mobile AI conversation experience, though consumer-growth metrics are absent.
TLDR: Solid SaaS/brand designer with AI-enabled workflow and payment-logic thinking, but product metrics are limited.
TLDR: Reliable agency UI/system designer for finance/health platforms, but evidence is more delivery than growth strategy.
TLDR: Senior UI/UX strategist with AI-research workflow and platform design breadth, but consumer monetization fit needs proof.
TLDR: Solid app/product designer with SKT youth-safety and digital-detox ownership, but limited metric evidence.
TLDR: Experienced UI/web/app generalist with PM/PL language, but the extract lacks product metrics.
TLDR: Strong commerce/platform UX candidate with LotteON and creator-home revenue impact.
TLDR: Strong commerce UX/UI candidate with mobile optimization, A/B testing, and revenue results.
TLDR: Performance-oriented UX/content designer with business lifts, but product depth is not fully clear.
TLDR: Broad service UI designer with ERP/homepage/member-app work, but no strong Podo-specific metric signal.
TLDR: Strong research-led UX candidate with concrete conversion/entry-flow metrics and design-system collaboration.
TLDR: Strong funnel/product designer with PG payment UX and clear conversion/retention gains.
TLDR: Very relevant dating-app UIUX designer; main concern is seniority and metric depth.
TLDR: Multi-platform UIUX designer with web/mobile/app/kiosk breadth, but evidence is more execution than product strategy.
TLDR: Senior web/UIUX designer with enterprise platform depth, but likely less consumer-product focused.
TLDR: Experienced finance/retail UIUX PL with mobile app and system work, but no growth/social evidence in the extract.
TLDR: Relevant app/UI designer with AI and accessibility-oriented public projects, but metric/product strategy evidence is modest.
TLDR: Strong education/product candidate with data-course conversion metrics and design-system efficiency gains.
TLDR: Promising junior with service-launch and community practice, but below the senior bar.
TLDR: Mid-level web/UI designer with app-project exposure, but not enough evidence for Podo's product/growth needs.
TLDR: Low-confidence fit; has B2B mobile/UIUX practice but the evidence is thin and junior-leaning.
TLDR: Solid high-ownership UIUX designer for education/self-publishing products, but metric evidence is missing.
TLDR: Strong UX planner for large consumer marketplace/community surfaces; promising for Podo if UI craft is strong.
TLDR: Strong enterprise/mobile product designer with AI-manufacturing and public app outcomes, but social/subscription fit is unclear.
TLDR: Execution-focused UI designer with Hyundai Capital and Qpicker app work, but limited product strategy evidence.
TLDR: Strong hands-on UX/UI candidate with onboarding impact and research ownership.
TLDR: Strong AI/service-brand candidate with research, A/B testing, and monetization examples.
TLDR: Strong UX/product candidate for onboarding, value perception, design systems, and community features.
TLDR: Experienced UI designer with app renewals and design guides, but many examples are web/project delivery rather than product growth.
TLDR: Growth/content-oriented designer with some app UI, but not yet a strong product-design match.
TLDR: Very strong domain fit: led product design for a reward-based dating app renewal.
TLDR: Early UXUI candidate with fine-art/visual strengths and methodology language, but little shipped product evidence.
TLDR: Product/UI designer with kiosk/POS/order-app experience, but the portfolio extraction is badly garbled.
